subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Cosmic Dark Ages
description: A quick, practical guide to The Cosmic Dark Ages.

hook.text: After the glow faded, the universe went dark. No stars existed yet. This era lasted 200 million years.

buildup.text: Hydrogen gas filled space in a uniform fog. Gravity slowly pulled it into denser clumps over millions of years.

discovery.text: The first stars eventually ignited, ending the Dark Ages. They were massive—hundreds of times larger than the Sun.

twist.text: These first stars contained no metals—only hydrogen and helium. They burned hot, lived fast, and died as supernovae.

climax.text: Their explosions seeded space with heavy elements, enabling the rocky planets and life that came later.

punchline.text: Two hundred million years of darkness. Then the first stars broke through.

quiz.question: Why were the Cosmic Dark Ages dark?
options: No stars had formed yet to produce visible light
Dust clouds blocked all light from reaching observers
The universe had expanded beyond the visible spectrum
